volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Seven against Thebes (Section 1.968 - Section 1059)

sections.label: Section 1.968
sections.translation: My heart groans within me .

sections.label: Section 1.969
sections.translation: Ah , the grief , brother all-lamentable .

sections.label: Section 1.970
sections.translation: And you also , brother all-wretched .

sections.label: Section 1.971
sections.translation: You perished at the hands of your nearest and dearest .

sections.label: Section 1.972
sections.translation: And you killed your nearest and dearest .

sections.label: Section 1.973
sections.translation: Twofold to tell of —

sections.label: Section 1.974
sections.translation: Twofold to look upon —

sections.label: Section 1.975
sections.translation: Are these sorrows so close to those .

sections.label: Section 1.976
sections.translation: Fraternal sorrows stand close by fraternal sorrows .

sections.label: Section 1.977
sections.translation: O Fate , giver of grievous troubles , and awful shade of Oedipus , black Erinys , you are indeed a mighty force .

sections.label: Section 1.980b
sections.translation: Ah , me

sections.label: Section 1.981
sections.translation: Sorrows hard to behold —

sections.label: Section 1.982
sections.translation: He showed me when he returned from exile .

sections.label: Section 1.983
sections.translation: But he made no return after he had killed .

sections.label: Section 1.984
sections.translation: He was saved , but lost his life .

sections.label: Section 1.985
sections.translation: He lost it , all too truly .

sections.label: Section 1.986
sections.translation: And took this one ’ s life away .

sections.label: Section 1.987
sections.translation: Wretched family !

sections.label: Section 1.988
sections.translation: Wretched suffering !

sections.label: Section 1.989
sections.translation: Kindred sorrows full of groans !

sections.label: Section 1.990
sections.translation: Sorrows steeped in tripled griefs .

sections.label: Section 1.991
sections.translation: O Fate , giver of grievous troubles , and awful shade of Oedipus , black Erinys , you are indeed a mighty force .

sections.label: Section 994
sections.translation: Now you know of the Erinys by experience —

sections.label: Section 995
sections.translation: And you are made aware no later —

sections.label: Section 996
sections.translation: When you came back to our city ,

sections.label: Section 997
sections.translation: Yes , to face him with your spear .

sections.label: Section 998
sections.translation: A tale of destruction !

sections.label: Section 999
sections.translation: Destruction to look upon !

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.translation: Oh , the grief —

sections.label: Section 1001
sections.translation: Oh , the evils —

sections.label: Section 1002
sections.original: δώμασι καὶ χθονί , [ πρὸ πάντων δ ἐμοί [*] ].
sections.translation: For home and land .

sections.label: Section 1003
sections.translation: Above all for me ,

sections.label: Section 1004
sections.translation: And more also for me .

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.translation: Ah I pity your grievous suffering , my king .

sections.label: Section 1006
sections.translation: Pity for you both , most lamentable of all men .

sections.label: Section 1007
sections.translation: You were possessed by delusion .

sections.label: Section 1008
sections.translation: Where shall we lay them in the earth ?

sections.label: Section 1009
sections.translation: Ah , where their honor is greatest .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: δοκοῦντα καὶ δόξαντ ἀπαγγέλλειν με χρὴ
sections.translation: To lie beside their father , a cause for him of sorrow .

sections.label: Section 1011
sections.translation:
It is my duty to announce the will and decrees of the council on behalf of the people of this our Cadmean city .
It is decreed , first , that Eteocles here , on account of his goodwill towards the city , is to be buried in a kindly grave in its soil ;

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: ἱερῶν πατρῴων δ ὅσιος ὢν μομφῆς ἄτερ
sections.translation: for hating the enemy he chose death in the city and driven by piety towards his ancestral shrines , he died without reproach where it is an honor for the young to die . This is how I was commanded to speak regarding him . But as for his brother , it is decreed that this corpse of Polyneices

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.translation: is to be cast out of the city unburied to be torn by dogs , since he would have been the destroyer of the land of the Cadmeans , if one of the gods had not used his brother ’ s spear to prevent him . Even in death he will retain the stain of his guilt against his fathers ’ gods , whom he dishonored

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: οὕτω πετηνῶν τόνδ ὑπ οἰωνῶν δοκεῖ
sections.translation: when he launched a foreign army against the city to take it . For this reason it is decreed that he will receive his reward by being buried without honor beneath the winged birds ; and that no labor of the hands shall attend him by building up a burial mound nor shall anyone offer him reverence in shrill-sung laments .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.translation: He is to be refused the honor of being carried in funeral procession by his loved ones . Such is the decree of the Cadmean authorities .

sections.label: Section 1032
sections.original: ἢν μή τις ἄλλος τόνδε συνθάπτειν θέλῃ , [*]
sections.translation: I at least will say something to the rulers of the Cadmeans : even if no one else is willing to share in burying him , I will bury him alone and risk the peril

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.translation: of burying my own brother . Nor am I ashamed to act in defiant opposition to the rulers of the city . A thing to be held in awe is the common womb from which we were born , of a wretched mother and unfortunate father . Therefore , my soul , willingly share his evils , even though they are unwilling ,

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.translation: and live in kindred spirit with the dead . No hollow-bellied wolves will tear his flesh — let no one decree that ! Even though I am a woman , I will myself find the means to give him burial and a grave ,

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: καὐτὴ καλύψω , μηδέ τῳ δόξῃ πάλιν :
sections.translation: carrying the earth in the fold of my linen robe . With my own hands I will cover him over — let no one decree it otherwise . Take heart , I will have the means to do it .

sections.label: Section 1048
sections.original: αὐδῶ σε μὴ περισσὰ κηρύσσειν ἐμοί .
sections.translation: I forbid you to act thus in violation of the city .

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: τραχύς γε μέντοι δῆμος ἐκφυγὼν κακά .
sections.translation: I forbid you to make useless proclamations to me .

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: τράχυν : ἄθαπτος δ οὗτος οὐ γενήσεται .
sections.translation: And yet a citizenry that has escaped evil can be harsh .

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: ἀλλ ὃν πόλις στυγεῖ , σὺ τιμήσεις τάφῳ ;
sections.translation: Let it be harsh ! This man will not be unburied .

sections.label: Section 1052
sections.translation: What ! Will you honor with burial a man whom the city detests ?

sections.label: Section 1053
sections.original: οὔ , πρίν γε χώραν τήνδε κινδύνῳ βαλεῖν .
sections.translation: For a long time now the gods have ceased to hold him in honor .

sections.label: Section 1054
sections.translation: No , he was honored until he put this land in jeopardy .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: ἀλλ εἰς ἅπαντας ἀνθ ἑνὸς τόδ ἔργον ἦν .
sections.translation: He suffered evil and gave evil in return .

sections.label: Section 1056
sections.translation: But this act was against all the citizens , not only one man .

sections.label: Section 1057
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ θάψω τόνδε : μὴ μακρηγόρει .
sections.translation: Discord is the last of the gods to close an argument . I will bury him . Put an end to your big talk .

sections.label: Section 1058
sections.original: ἀλλ αὐτόβουλος ἴσθ , ἀπεννέπω δ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Well then , follow your own rash plan , but I forbid it . Exit .

sections.label: Section 1059
sections.translation: Ah , misery !
